linguistic_leave_route.value: Luxembourg linguistic leave is capped at 200 hours in two stages, and eligible Luxembourgish courses must come from recognised or approved provider categories.

official_certification_context.value: INLL is the national certification body for Luxembourgish and the official Sproochentest provider, but the FAQ says the article-28 nationality courses are organised by the Adult Education Service or its contracted providers.

value: INLL free self-learning route for workplace Luxembourgish
A provider-issued CEFR-style certificate should not be marketed as automatically sufficient for official needs.
The narrow safe fact is that INLL does not offer the specific nationality-law article-28 courses; broader certificate-validity wording remains unsafe.
Only a free public self-learning route is evidenced for INLL; overcrowding and genericity are not.
No primary evidence in the rerun proves rising Luxembourgish demand for this exact product.

target_audience_summary.value: Luxembourg employees and employers needing workplace Luxembourgish while navigating official leave and recognition constraints.
target_audience_summary.omission_reason: Useful synthesis, but still partly inferential for the exact product.

official_sufficiency_of_product_certific.omission_reason: The rerun does not support treating a product certificate as automatically valid for official needs.

inll_overcrowding_or_genericity.omission_reason: Only the existence of a free public route is evidenced.

demand_growth_story.omission_reason: No primary demand-growth metric survived the rerun.
